title: Why accounting exists: stakeholders & the accounting equation

questions.explanation: Financial accounting exists to give external decision-makers a standardized, trustworthy view of a business they cannot observe directly.
A bank is an external stakeholder relying on financial statements; the others are internal users served by managerial accounting.
Assets = Liabilities + Equity. Resources controlled equal the claims against them (creditors + owners).
Equity is the owners' residual claim: Equity = Assets − Liabilities, i.e. what remains after creditors are satisfied.
Borrowing $30k cash raises assets (cash) by $30,000 and liabilities by $30,000; total assets change by +$30,000.
A stakeholder is any person or group with an interest in the business's performance.
Paying cash for equipment swaps one asset for another: equipment +$20k, cash −$20k. Total assets and the equation are unchanged.
